lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS224N: NLP with Deep Learning | Spring 2024 | Lecture 5 - Recurrent Neural Networks

unit.name: Fixed-window neural language models and why they fall short
unit.description: Introduces the first neural alternative to n-grams, then explains why a fixed-window architecture still misses the full language-modeling problem.
unit.summary: The lecture next replaces count tables with a neural classifier: concatenate embeddings from a fixed context window, pass them through a hidden layer, and apply a softmax over the vocabulary to get a next-word distribution [ts=3032s] [ts=3123s] [ts=3178s]. This early neural language model, associated with Bengio and colleagues, removes explicit sparsity and storage problems because the model stores learned parameters instead of all observed n-grams [ts=3205s] [ts=3212s] [ts=3303s]. But it still uses a Markov-style fixed window, and its position-specific weight matrix treats the same word differently depending on where it appears, motivating a shared-parameter sequence model instead [ts=3328s] [ts=3351s] [ts=3410s].

key_points.text: A fixed-window neural language model concatenates word embeddings from a local context and predicts the next word with a softmax classifier.
This architecture is essentially the first neural language model associated with early 2000s work by Bengio and colleagues.
Neural language models remove the need to store all observed n-grams explicitly.
The fixed-window model still makes a Markov assumption because it throws away far-back context.
The same word occurring in different input positions is handled by different subparts of the weight matrix, which is inefficient.
These limitations motivate a variable-length, shared-parameter architecture: the recurrent neural network.

kp_catalog.name: Fixed-window neural language models
kp_catalog.description: Neural next-word predictors that embed a limited context window and classify the next token with a softmax.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fixed-window neural language models clarifies neural next-word predictors that embed a limited context window and classify the next token with a softmax, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: N-gram language models
kp_catalog.description: Count-based next-word models that use a short Markov context, smoothing, and backoff.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because n-gram language models clarifies count-based next-word models that use a short Markov context, smoothing, and backoff, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent neural networks
kp_catalog.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context. Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent neural networks formalizes how shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
